title: Understanding Agency in Woman's Economic Empowerment Policy: an Analysis of Three Programs in India
reasoning_research: The study applies an adapted framework to analyze policy texts, demonstrating a clear methodological approach, but lacks details on sample selection criteria, analytical robustness checks, or any mention of preregistration. The conceptual contribution is primarily in applying an existing empowerment framework to policy analysis rather than introducing novel theory or measurement.
reasoning_impact: The work is relevant to understanding how agency concepts are embedded in economic policies, which could inform program design in development contexts. Its impact within decision neuroscience or computational psychology is limited, as it focuses on policy text analysis rather than cognitive mechanisms, learning, or neural processes of decision-making.
